content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901           322-6441           322-6444

January 21, 1983


Betsy Strauss
City Attorney
City of Napa
Napa, CA 94558

Re: Telephone Advice Request
     Our File No. A-83-008

Dear Ms. Strauss:

Thank you for your telephone request for our advice on the
interpretation of the Levine Bill, AB 1040, Chap. 1049, Stats.
1982. You have correctly applied the regulations approved by
the Commission on December 21, 1982, to implement the new
Government Code Section 84308.* You have given me the following
facts:

FACTS

The City of Napa has a mobilehome rent review commission.
The commission is appointed by the city council and consists of
five city councilmembers plus two "public" members, one a
mobilehome park owner the other a mobilehome park tenant. The
commission reviews mobilehome park rents. It is thus a
quasi-judicial board or commission.

ANALYSIS

Government Code Section 84308(d) exempts from the section's
restrictions "legislative bodies such as city councils, county
boards of supervisors," etc., "while acting as a body or a
committee thereof." In the Commission's recently approved

*This regulation has not yet been filed with the Secretary
of State. Although it is not yet effective as a regulation, we
are using it as a guideline for interpreting Section 84308
whenever we are asked for advice under Section 83114.

Betsy Strauss
January 21, 1983
Page 2


regulation,* 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.1, these terms are
defined, as follows:

     (a)  A "legislative body" is excluded by
Government Code Section 84308(d) from the coverage of
Government Code Sections 84308(a), (b), and (c) if its
entire membership consists of elected officers directly
elected by the voters to serve on that body.  As used
herein, the term "voters" has the same meaning as in
Elections Code Section 18.

     (b)  A legislative body is "acting as a body or a
committee thereof" when:

          (1)  It acts in its entirety as itself or as
the ex officio governing body of any district or
other entity; or

          (2)  Any subgroup of the legislative body
composed solely of members of the body sits by
designation of the body.

     (c)  A "quasi-judicial board or commission" means
any appointed or elected governmental body, other than
a legislative body, which may make decisions concerning
licenses, permits, or other entitlements for use.  A
quasi-judicial board or commission may consist of a
single public official if that official exercises final
decision-making authority for his or her agency.

     The City Council of Napa is, by definition in the statute, a
"legislative body."  However, it is not "acting as a body or
committee thereof" when its members combine with anyone else to
make up another body.  Such is the case with the Napa rent
review commission.  Consequently, the Napa City Councilmembers,
when sitting on this commission, are subject to the provisions
of Government Code Section 84308, as are the other members of
the Commission.

     To further illustrate this rule, we discussed the examples
of a Local Agency Formation Commission (LAFCO) which consists of
a mix of public officials, including various members of
"legislative bodies."  The LAFCO board is not exempted by
Government Code Section 84308(d) and 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18438.1(b).  The same is true of many regional boards or

*Id.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether city councilmembers serving on a mobilehome rent review commission that includes both elected officials and public members are subject to Government Code Section 84308 restrictions, or whether they are exempt as members of a legislative body.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Napa City Councilmembers are subject to Government Code Section 84308 provisions when sitting on the mobilehome rent review commission because the commission is not a "legislative body acting as a body or committee thereof" but rather a quasi-judicial board composed of a mix of elected officials and public members. The exemption for legislative bodies under Section 84308(d) does not apply when legislative body members combine with others to form a different governing body.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Telephone advice request letter dated January 21, 1983. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections, but analysis clearly presents the legal question and conclusion. The document includes defined facts and detailed analysis applying regulatory definitions. File number A-83-008.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether city councilmembers serving on a mobilehome rent review commission that includes both elected officials and public members are subject to Government Code Section 84308 restrictions, or whether they are exempt as members of a legislative body.

CONCLUSION: The Napa City Councilmembers are subject to Government Code Section 84308 provisions when sitting on the mobilehome rent review commission because the commission is not a "legislative body acting as a body or committee thereof" but rather a quasi-judicial board composed of a mix of elected officials and public members. The exemption for legislative bodies under Section 84308(d) does not apply when legislative body members combine with others to form a different governing body.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter regarding the application of AB 1040 (Government Code Section 84308) to city councilmembers serving on a mobilehome rent review commission in Napa. The FPPC concludes that councilmembers are subject to Section 84308 restrictions when serving on this quasi-judicial commission because it is not a legislative body acting as itself.
